top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Three big tensions define today's news. The U.S.-Iran war drags into day 80 with Tehran teasing a Strait of Hormuz toll plan even as Trump warns of a 'very bad time' if no deal lands soon. The Russia-Ukraine truce has fully collapsed - Moscow refused to extend it and both sides are back to firing hundreds of drones a night. And the much-hyped Trump-Xi summit ended with little to show beyond a Boeing order, leaving markets nervous about the wider tariff picture. Quieter day for Singapore - mostly local policy and weather.

stories.headline: Iran prepares to unveil Strait of Hormuz toll plan as Trump warns of 'very bad time'
stories.summary: Tehran says it will soon roll out a 'professional mechanism' to manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and charge fees for vessels using a designated route. Iran's First VP said 'enemy' military equipment will no longer be allowed through. Trump has warned Tehran of a 'very bad time' if no peace deal is reached. PBS and U.S. officials say the toll idea violates basic norms of freedom of navigation - around 20% of global oil moves through the strait.

stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine truce collapses; both sides resume mass drone strikes
stories.summary: The U.S.-brokered 72-hour ceasefire announced by Trump has expired and Moscow refused Kyiv's offer to extend it. Russia launched 287 attack drones overnight (279 intercepted), while Ukraine returned the favour with one of its biggest waves yet - debris hit Moscow's Vnukovo airport and at least four people died. Zelenskyy says 'Russia has no intention of ending this war,' contradicting Putin's earlier suggestion that the conflict was 'coming to an end.'

stories.headline: Trump-Xi summit ends with Boeing order but little else concrete
stories.summary: Trump wrapped up three days in Beijing claiming 'fantastic trade deals,' but the only firm number was a Chinese commitment to buy 200 Boeing planes (Boeing's stock fell 4% on the news as markets had hoped for more). The two sides also flagged $17B/year in U.S. ag purchases through 2028 and a Chinese pledge to ease rare-earth supply. Beijing's and Washington's readouts disagree on what was actually agreed, leaving the wider tariff standoff effectively unchanged.

stories.headline: Asia's economic divergence: AI boom in some markets, oil shock pain in others
stories.summary: CNN Business reports the historic oil shock from the Iran war is splitting Asia in two - tech-heavy economies riding the AI wave keep growing while fuel-importing nations face shortages and price spikes that risk humanitarian fallout. Trump returned from China to rising U.S. inflation as well. Analysts warn the divergence is a global warning sign of how fragile growth has become.

stories.headline: Honda pauses Canada EV plant, Cuba runs out of diesel - signs of shifting energy reality
stories.summary: Honda has suspended plans for a major new EV plant in Ontario, blaming weak EV demand and U.S. tariffs on Canadian-made cars. Meanwhile Cuba says it has run completely out of diesel because of the U.S. blockade, with Havana blackouts now stretching 20-22 hours a day. The two stories sit at opposite ends of an industrial world wrestling with tariffs, sanctions and an Iran-driven oil crunch.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones launched from Iraqi airspace
stories.summary: The Saudi defence ministry said three hostile drones launched from Iraqi airspace were shot down over the kingdom on May 17. No injuries were reported. The incident is the latest sign that Iran-aligned militias in Iraq are still active despite the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, and adds another front to the regional pressure on Riyadh.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Quiet Sunday-into-Monday for Singapore but a couple of policy shifts worth knowing. From this weekend, first-time drug abusers who turn themselves in skip detention and go straight onto community supervision - a notable softening of the city-state's famously tough approach. Diesel prices keep climbing on the back of the Iran war, with private ambulance operators now flagging possible fuel surcharges. Expect short, sharp afternoon thunderstorms today; the regulator IMDA is also probing alleged spectrum breaches at Simba that have stalled Keppel's M1 sale.

stories.headline: First-time drug abusers who surrender will no longer be detained, MHA confirms
stories.summary: Changes that took effect on Saturday mean first-time drug abusers in Singapore who surrender themselves will skip detention at the Drug Rehabilitation Centre. Instead they go on mandatory community-based drug supervision with case management. Authorities say the move recognises that abusers are getting 'younger and younger' and that early intervention works better than incarceration for first-timers.

stories.headline: LRT disrupted between Bukit Panjang and Senja after man falls onto track
stories.summary: There was no LRT service between Bukit Panjang and Senja stations after a man fell onto the track. Bridging bus services were activated and SMRT and LTA are investigating. Commuters in the north-west are urged to plan extra time today.

stories.headline: Private ambulance operators weigh fuel surcharges as diesel jumps 70%
stories.summary: Private ambulance operators in Singapore say they are considering fuel surcharges as diesel prices have surged about 70% on the back of the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Most non-emergency patient transfers in Singapore are run by private operators, so a surcharge would feed straight into hospital and elderly-care bills.

stories.headline: IMDA probes Simba over alleged spectrum breaches; Keppel-M1 sale stalls
stories.summary: Keppel's planned sale of its M1 stake has stalled while the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) investigates alleged spectrum-licence breaches by rival telco Simba. The probe creates uncertainty around the telco market just as M1 was looking for a new owner; analysts say a resolution could take weeks.

stories.headline: Afternoon thunderstorms expected across Singapore through the rest of May
stories.summary: NEA says short-duration thundery showers are expected on most afternoons through the second half of May 2026, occasionally stretching into the evening. Today's outlook: 33C high, 26C low, 55-60% chance of afternoon thunderstorms. Commuters should plan for sudden downpours - PUB flood alerts are not active right now but low-lying areas should stay alert.

stories.headline: Polyclinic wait times and HDB resale prices on Parliament agenda
stories.summary: From the May 7 Order Paper, MPs are pressing the government on two cost-of-living pinch points: HDB resale prices (Mr Victor Lye asking whether the recent moderation is broad-based and what more is being done to help first-time buyers near family/work) and long polyclinic walk-in waits during peak hours (Mr Vikram Nair asking whether load can be spread to off-peak slots).

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three big theatres got worse this weekend. The Russia-Ukraine truce collapsed and both sides went straight back to mass drone warfare, with Moscow's biggest airport hit. Israel re-opened airstrikes on southern Lebanon and killed Hamas's military-wing commander in Gaza, even as a separate Lebanon ceasefire technically still holds. And in the Gulf, Iran is doubling down on its Strait of Hormuz toll threat while Trump warns of a 'very bad time' - day 80 of the U.S./Israel-Iran war with no ceasefire in sight.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - ceasefire expired May 17, mass drone strikes resumed

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
U.S. begins pushing both sides toward a negotiated settlement; talks stall through Q1 2026.
Start of the four-week period in which Russia would net-lose 45 sq miles of Ukrainian territory.
Russia and Ukraine declare competing ceasefires - neither holds.
Putin suggests war is 'coming to an end'; Zelenskyy disagrees publicly.
Truce expires; Russia fires 200+ drones overnight at Ukraine.
Russia-Ukraine War Report Card notes record monthly Russian drone use (8,000+ in April).
Trump announces a fresh 72-hour U.S.-brokered truce, calling it 'the beginning of the end.'
Large-scale Ukrainian drone strike hits Moscow region overnight; multiple explosions reported.

Truce expires. Moscow refuses Kyiv's offer to extend. Ukraine launches one of its biggest drone waves yet - debris hits Vnukovo, Moscow's largest airport, killing at least 4 people. Russia fires 287 attack drones at Ukraine (279 intercepted).
Ukraine strikes gas facilities in Russia's Orenburg region, 1,500km from the border - Zelenskyy calls it retaliation for May 13-14 Kyiv strikes.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran War & Strait of Hormuz Crisis
theatres.status: Active - ceasefire technically in force since April 7-8 but Hormuz crisis escalating

timeline.event: Original 12-Day War between Israel and Iran, including U.S. strikes on Iranian nuclear sites; first ceasefire holds for months.
U.S. and Israel launch joint airstrikes on Iran; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed along with several officials. The 2026 Iran war begins.
U.S. and Iran agree to a ceasefire including Israel after five weeks of fighting.
Trump extends the ceasefire on day 55; tensions shift to Strait of Hormuz access.
Tehran signals it is ready for more U.S. talks; clashes continue in Hormuz.
Iran announces a new system to collect fees from ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
Tehran says it will 'unveil Hormuz toll mechanism soon'; Lebanon truce extended on the same day.

Iran war day 79: Iran's First VP says 'enemy' military gear can no longer pass through the strait; Trump warns of a 'very bad time' if no deal is reached. Pakistani interior minister flies to Tehran to mediate.
Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones launched from Iraqi airspace - suggesting Iran-aligned militias remain active.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Active - April 17 Lebanon ceasefire technically holding but hundreds killed in violations

timeline.event: Hamas attack on Israel; Israel launches Gaza war.
Escalation between Hezbollah and Israel grows into the 2026 Lebanon war; over 2,000 killed.
Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon during the broader Iran war push casualties higher.
Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes effect - but fighting continues with hundreds killed since.
Hezbollah and Israel swap threats and cross-border strikes.
More than a dozen killed in Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon.
Lebanon-Israel truce formally extended; Tehran signals readiness for more U.S. talks.
Izz-al-Din al-Haddad, commander of Hamas's military wing, killed in an Israeli strike in western Gaza City. Hamas calls the loss 'profound.'

IDF issues evacuation warnings for four villages in southern Lebanon (Irzay, Merouaniyeh, Babliyeh, Baisariyah) ahead of new airstrikes. Hezbollah drone intercepted; rockets fired at IDF troops also intercepted. At least 5 killed in Israeli strikes across south and east Lebanon.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak is reaching its endgame. The ship is docking in the Netherlands today (18 May) - that closes the on-board phase and shifts the response entirely to land-based contact tracing across the 12 countries already holding former passengers. WHO still rates the global risk as 'low' but the case count keeps creeping up: 11 confirmed cases and 3 deaths as of mid-week, with two new confirmed cases from France and Spain after repatriation. The Andes virus is the only hantavirus known to spread person-to-person, which is why every new case matters.

stories.headline: MV Hondius docks in the Netherlands today, ending on-board phase of outbreak
stories.summary: The Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius is scheduled to arrive at its final stop in the Netherlands on 18 May 2026. Dutch authorities will run final disinfection, contact tracing on remaining crew, and lab confirmation of any unresolved cases. After today, the outbreak response shifts entirely to the 12 countries holding former passengers.

stories.headline: WHO and ECDC confirm 11 cases, 3 deaths; two new confirmed cases after repatriation
stories.summary: WHO's latest count is 11 confirmed cases as of 13 May. ECDC reports 8 confirmed and 2 suspected cases directly linked, with 3 deaths (2 confirmed Andes virus). Since 8 May, two more confirmed cases have been identified - one from France who became symptomatic during repatriation, one from Spain who tested positive on arrival but is asymptomatic. A US case has inconclusive results.

stories.headline: Former passengers under monitoring across 12 countries - including Singapore
stories.summary: As of 15 May, former MV Hondius passengers are hospitalised or quarantined in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey, and the U.S. Singapore's CDA is monitoring its returnee(s) but has not raised the public alert level. WHO still rates the broader public health risk as 'low.'

stories.headline: CDC reminds public Andes virus can spread between humans
stories.summary: The U.S. CDC's latest update on the M/V Hondius cluster reiterates that Andes virus is the only known hantavirus capable of person-to-person spread - through close, sustained contact, and possibly by airborne droplets. Most hantaviruses only spread from rodents, so this is the specific feature making the cruise-ship cluster unusual.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: The cycle is dominated by network-edge devices and Microsoft. CISA's deadline to patch a critical Cisco SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) hit yesterday and the bug is being weaponised in the wild by the UAT-8616 group. Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday cleared 120 flaws including a critical Windows DNS RCE and an ASP.NET Core privilege-escalation, while two Windows BitLocker/CTFMON zero-days ('YellowKey' and 'GreenPlasma') are under active attack. And in a first, Google says it caught a hacker group using AI to develop a 2FA-bypass zero-day for mass exploitation - a milestone for AI-assisted exploit dev.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) hits CISA KEV deadline
stories.summary: CISA's federal-agency deadline to patch a critical Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication-bypass passed on 17 May 2026. The bug lets an unauthenticated remote attacker bypass auth and gain admin on affected systems. Cisco attributes active exploitation to threat cluster UAT-8616 - the same group behind weaponisation of CVE-2026-20127.

stories.headline: Microsoft May Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws; 17 critical, 14 are remote code execution
stories.summary: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 120 vulnerabilities including 17 critical, 14 of them remote code execution. The headline issue is CVE-2026-41096, a Windows DNS Client RCE where an attacker-controlled DNS server can run code on the victim via a crafted response. No zero-days in this batch, but admins should still treat it as urgent.

stories.headline: Critical ASP.NET Core flaw lets unauth attackers gain SYSTEM by forging cookies
stories.summary: Microsoft released emergency security updates for CVE-2026-40372, a critical flaw in ASP.NET Core's Data Protection cryptographic APIs that lets an unauthenticated attacker forge authentication cookies and gain SYSTEM-level privileges. Every internet-facing ASP.NET Core app should patch immediately.

stories.headline: Windows zero-days 'YellowKey' and 'GreenPlasma' actively exploited (BitLocker bypass + CTFMON LPE)
stories.summary: An anonymous researcher disclosed two Windows zero-days under active exploitation: 'YellowKey' bypasses BitLocker to read protected drives, and 'GreenPlasma' escalates privileges via the Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON). A proof-of-concept for the BitLocker bypass is already public. No CVEs assigned yet; Microsoft says it is investigating.

stories.headline: Google: hacker group used AI to build first-known zero-day 2FA bypass for mass exploitation
stories.summary: Google Threat Intelligence Group says it identified an unknown actor using a zero-day exploit 'likely developed with an AI system' to bypass two-factor authentication in a popular open-source sysadmin tool. Google says it thwarted what could have been a mass-exploitation event. It is the first publicly documented in-the-wild case of AI being used to discover and weaponise a vulnerability.

stories.headline: CISA also flags VMware Aria Operations RCE (CVE-2026-22719) as exploited
stories.summary: CISA added a VMware Aria Operations remote code execution flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, confirming in-the-wild attacks. Enterprises running Aria Operations should patch immediately and review logs for suspicious admin actions.
